results.title: Socratic: Intelligent task management for modern software teams | Product Hunt
results.content:
Socratic started from a single, lasting frustration. Every task tool we've ever used was high tax, low return. In those tools, you do all the data entry, and then you do all the sorting, sifting, and spelunking to try to understand the state of work. We wondered: what if we flipped the equation? What if we made a task system that works for you? What if we built something that wasn't just Jira with nicer lipstick, something that was much more than (as one early Socratic user described Jira), "A form UI over a database that tells me nothing about the way we work"? Enter Socratic. Socratic harnesses your work activity and shows ways of working easier, faster, better—all with no manual inputs. Your daily activity drives Socratic's insights; real-time insights drive stronger work health. Think [...] Report

Brad Hipps

Socratic

Socratic

Maker

@jamie\_dowis Understood! We actually tried in a previous life to do what you suggest--essentially, to "overlay" existing task tools--and the results weren't great. It's tough to provide users actionable intelligence, at the time they most need it, when you aren't the system where the work is actually being done. That said, we do integrate with git (GitHub, GitLab), not only to automate task movement, but to enrich our own work intelligence with analytics we build from code activity. We will definitely do the same with other complementary systems in software dev-- CI/CD comes to mind.

Report

Roop Reddy

Congratulations on the launch! Every time I use Jira, I always think why there is no better tool than Jira. This looks like a solution [...] Report

I love the analytics that Socratic shows me, helps me understand my team and my workflows. As a PM, I love how the interface doesn’t get in my way, it just lets me do my work. And it makes my sprint planning and sprint review super easy. Congrats team!

Report

Taylor Dahlem

@boney\_yeldho Music to our ears!!

Report

Dixon Hunt

Awesome product that helps you track conversations in order to highlight improvements for future sales calls. Would highly recommended!

Report [...] Thank you, Product Hunters!! 🙏 How we got here (in third person, narrated by Morgan Freeman): Like most 8 year olds in the early 2000’s, Birch Eve spent most of his free time playing Xbox Live with total strangers across the globe. Considering we had just progressed as a society from dial-up, the internet was spotty and frequently went out. So, naturally, he called the local, friendly corporation of AT&T himself. The first thing he heard every single time? “This call may be monitored and recorded…” Fast forward to a year ago, Birch made a similar call, and that exact automated phrase was repeated back to him. “Why do companies still need to do this? How are companies using these recordings? How can we leverage these conversations to be more customer focused?” Out of these questions, [...] 👋🏼 What up, Product Hunt! We are so excited to share what we’ve been working on! So, what's the big dill? (pun intended): Sales teams each have hundreds or thousands of phone and video conversations every day. There are millions of points of data hidden inside every single one, but there has never been an efficient way to extract and consume that data. With Pickle, you don’t have to choose call recordings at random and listen to them for hours on end. We make it easy for you to review calls in seconds, while also delivering actionable insights to your inbox every day. The Pickle Juice (or, Bread and Butter): Every company has different customers and different priorities, so it’s important to be able to customize how your conversations are analyzed. In Pickle you can receive insights based
